data.title: 3 TSX Companies Estimated To Be Priced Below Their Intrinsic Values
data.description:
Amid a barrage of geopolitical tensions and U.S. policy developments, the Canadian market has shown remarkable resilience, with the TSX hitting record highs as fundamentals like low energy prices and solid growth continue to anchor stability. In this environment, identifying stocks priced below their intrinsic values can offer investors potential opportunities; focusing on strong fundamentals becomes key in navigating these complex market dynamics.

Click here to see the full list of 22 stocks from our Undervalued TSX Stocks Based On Cash Flows screener.

Let's explore several standout options from the results in the screener.

Colliers International Group (TSX:CIGI)

Overview: Colliers International Group Inc. is a global provider of commercial real estate services to corporate and institutional clients across various regions, with a market cap of CA$10.11 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue segments include Engineering at $1.72 billion, Real Estate Services at $3.20 billion, and Investment Management at $525.24 million.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 22.4%

Colliers International Group is trading at CA$194.53, significantly below its future cash flow value estimate of CA$250.58, indicating it may be undervalued based on cash flows. Despite lower profit margins this year (2.1% vs 3.4%), earnings are forecast to grow substantially at 27.1% annually, outpacing the Canadian market's growth rate of 12.3%. However, debt coverage by operating cash flow remains a concern amidst significant insider selling recently noted in the company’s shares.

  • Our earnings growth report unveils the potential for significant increases in Colliers International Group's future results.
  • Click to explore a detailed breakdown of our findings in Colliers International Group's balance sheet health report.

Dexterra Group (TSX:DXT)

Overview: Dexterra Group Inc. provides support services for infrastructure creation, management, and operation in Canada with a market cap of CA$809.48 million.

Operations: The company's revenue is derived from two segments: Support Services, contributing CA$844.20 million, and Asset Based Services, generating CA$173.86 million.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 49.7%

Dexterra Group, trading at CA$13.06, is valued below its estimated future cash flow value of CA$25.94, highlighting potential undervaluation based on cash flows. Recent earnings show a rise in net income to CA$12.88 million for Q3 2025 from CA$7.66 million the previous year, with earnings expected to grow significantly at 20.64% annually—surpassing the Canadian market's growth rate of 12.3%. However, the company maintains a high level of debt which warrants attention.

  • Our growth report here indicates Dexterra Group may be poised for an improving outlook.
  • Unlock comprehensive insights into our analysis of Dexterra Group stock in this financial health report.

5N Plus (TSX:VNP)

Overview: 5N Plus Inc. is a company that produces and sells specialty semiconductors and performance materials across the Americas, Europe, Asia, and internationally, with a market cap of CA$1.68 billion.

Operations: The company's revenue is derived from two main segments: $98.86 million from performance materials and $261.09 million from specialty semiconductors.

Estimated Discount To Fair Value: 30.3%

5N Plus, trading at CA$19.02, is priced below its estimated future cash flow value of CA$27.28, suggesting potential undervaluation based on cash flows. The company reported a significant rise in net income to US$18.18 million for Q3 2025 from US$6.37 million the previous year, with earnings forecasted to grow at 15.6% annually—outpacing the Canadian market's growth rate of 12.4%. Recent index inclusions may enhance visibility and investor interest.

data.title: Why We're Not Concerned About Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance Company's (TADAWUL:8210) Share Price
data.description:
Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance Company's (TADAWUL:8210) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 21.6x might make it look like a sell right now compared to the market in Saudi Arabia, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 18x and even P/E's below 12x are quite common. However, the P/E might be high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

While the market has experienced earnings growth lately, Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance's earnings have gone into reverse gear, which is not great. It might be that many expect the dour earnings performance to recover substantially, which has kept the P/E from collapsing. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

View our latest analysis for Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance.
Advertisement

How Is Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance's Growth Trending?

In order to justify its P/E ratio, Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance would need to produce impressive growth in excess of the market.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered a frustrating 12% decrease to the company's bottom line. Still, the latest three year period has seen an excellent 59% overall rise in EPS, in spite of its unsatisfying short-term performance. So we can start by confirming that the company has generally done a very good job of growing earnings over that time, even though it had some hiccups along the way.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 14% per year during the coming three years according to the four analysts following the company. That's shaping up to be materially higher than the 11% per year growth forecast for the broader market.

In light of this, it's understandable that Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. It seems most investors are expecting this strong future growth and are willing to pay more for the stock.

What We Can Learn From Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance's P/E?

Using the price-to-earnings ratio alone to determine if you should sell your stock isn't sensible, however it can be a practical guide to the company's future prospects.

We've established that Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance maintains its high P/E on the strength of its forecast growth being higher than the wider market, as expected. Right now shareholders are comfortable with the P/E as they are quite confident future earnings aren't under threat. It's hard to see the share price falling strongly in the near future under these circumstances.

It is also worth noting that we have found 2 warning signs for Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance (1 is concerning!) that you need to take into consideration.

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking at a few good candidates. So take a peek at this free list of companies with a strong growth track record, trading on a low P/E.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Downgrade Puts NetApp Valuation And Growth In Focus
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley has downgraded NetApp (NasdaqGS:NTAP), citing a shift in enterprise technology spending.
  • The bank points to reduced budgets for storage hardware and higher memory costs as key risks for the company.
  • The reassessment highlights potential pressure on NetApp's growth and profitability outlook.

NetApp focuses on data storage and management solutions for enterprises, a segment closely tied to corporate IT budgets. When companies reassess their spending on storage hardware, providers like NetApp can feel the impact in demand for new systems and upgrades. Rising memory costs also matter because they can influence how expensive it is to build and sell storage products.

For you as an investor, this downgrade puts a spotlight on how sensitive NetApp may be to shifts in enterprise spending patterns and hardware input costs. It raises questions about how the company might balance pricing, profitability, and investment in its product portfolio if these pressures persist.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for NetApp by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on NetApp.

Why NetApp could be great value

Advertisement

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At US$99.25, NetApp trades about 19% below the US$122.81 analyst consensus target.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Our model views the shares as undervalued, trading roughly 46.2% below estimated fair value.
  • ❌ Recent Momentum: The stock has seen a 9.6% decline over the last 30 days.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for NetApp.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 Morgan Stanley's downgrade highlights pressure from tighter enterprise storage budgets and higher memory costs, which could influence sentiment even if valuation looks supportive.
  • 📊 Monitor gross margins, storage hardware demand trends, and any commentary on enterprise IT spending in upcoming updates.
  • ⚠️ A key risk is that prolonged cost inflation or weaker storage demand could weigh on profitability even if revenue holds steady.

Dig Deeper

data.title: Is Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) Attractive After Recent Share Price Softness?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Mid-America Apartment Communities at around US$134.66 is offering fair value or a potential gap between price and worth, you are in the right place.
  • The stock has seen a 0.6% decline over the past week, a 1.2% decline over the past month, and is down 3.2% year to date and 4.4% over the past year, while still sitting 21.0% above its level five years ago.
  • Recent attention on Mid-America Apartment Communities has focused on how its share price has softened over the last year while still being ahead over a five-year period. That mix of shorter term weakness and longer term gains has some investors reassessing whether the current price fairly reflects the underlying business.
  • Right now the company has a valuation score of 3 out of 6. In what follows, we look at what different valuation methods indicate about that score, then conclude with a broader way of thinking about value that goes beyond the usual models.

Mid-America Apartment Communities delivered -4.4% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Residential REITs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Mid-America Apartment Communities Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A DCF model takes the cash a business is expected to generate in the future, in this case adjusted funds from operations, and discounts those amounts back to what they could be worth today.

For Mid-America Apartment Communities, the latest twelve month free cash flow is about $952.8 million. Using a 2 stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolated estimates project free cash flow rising to $1,029.6 million by 2030, with a path of annual projections supplied for the next decade and later years extrapolated by Simply Wall St.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today and divided by the number of shares, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $191.89 per share.

Compared with the recent share price of around $134.66, this intrinsic value estimate suggests the stock is trading at roughly a 29.8% discount on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Mid-America Apartment Communities is undervalued by 29.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 880 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Mid-America Apartment Communities.

Approach 2: Mid-America Apartment Communities Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that share currently generates. It helps you see how many dollars of price the market is attaching to each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower P/E.

Mid-America Apartment Communities currently trades on a P/E of 28.55x. That sits above the Residential REITs industry average P/E of 20.56x, but below the 41.78x average of selected peers. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for the stock is 27.05x, which is its proprietary estimate of what the P/E "should" be given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and key risks.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for the company’s own characteristics rather than assuming all REITs or peers deserve the same multiple. On this basis, Mid-America Apartment Communities P/E of 28.55x sits modestly above the 27.05x Fair Ratio, which points to the shares looking slightly overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1427 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Mid-America Apartment Communities Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to think about value than a single P/E or DCF output, so let us introduce you to Narratives.

A Narrative is your story about a company, where you spell out how you see its future revenue, earnings and margins, then connect those assumptions to a fair value estimate instead of just accepting the market price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an easy tool that helps you link Mid-America Apartment Communities real world story to a financial forecast, and then to a fair value that you can compare directly with today’s share price to decide if you prefer to wait, add or trim.

Because Narratives on the platform are refreshed when new information such as earnings or news is added, your fair value view can stay aligned with the latest data rather than a once off model.

For example, one investor might build a Narrative for Mid-America Apartment Communities that results in a fair value far above US$134.66, while another might arrive at a fair value well below that level. This shows how the same stock can look attractive or expensive depending on the story you believe.

Do you think there's more to the story for Mid-America Apartment Communities? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Is CytomX Therapeutics, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:CTMX) Stock's Recent Performance Being Led By Its Attractive Financial Prospects?
data.description:
CytomX Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:CTMX) stock is up by a considerable 75% over the past three months. Given the company's impressive performance, we decided to study its financial indicators more closely as a company's financial health over the long-term usually dictates market outcomes. In this article, we decided to focus on CytomX Therapeutics' ROE.

Return on equity or ROE is an important factor to be considered by a shareholder because it tells them how effectively their capital is being reinvested. Simply put, it is used to assess the profitability of a company in relation to its equity capital.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for CytomX Therapeutics is:

26% = US$28m ÷ US$107m (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the profit over the last twelve months. Another way to think of that is that for every $1 worth of equity, the company was able to earn $0.26 in profit.

Check out our latest analysis for CytomX Therapeutics

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

We have already established that ROE serves as an efficient profit-generating gauge for a company's future earnings. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

CytomX Therapeutics' Earnings Growth And 26% ROE

Firstly, we acknowledge that CytomX Therapeutics has a significantly high ROE. Second, a comparison with the average ROE reported by the industry of 18% also doesn't go unnoticed by us. So, the substantial 50% net income growth seen by CytomX Therapeutics over the past five years isn't overly surprising.

We then compared CytomX Therapeutics' net income growth with the industry and we're pleased to see that the company's growth figure is higher when compared with the industry which has a growth rate of 22% in the same 5-year period.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. It’s important for an investor to know whether the market has priced in the company's expected earnings growth (or decline). This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. Is CytomX Therapeutics fairly valued compared to other companies? These 3 valuation measures might help you decide.

Is CytomX Therapeutics Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

CytomX Therapeutics doesn't pay any regular dividends currently which essentially means that it has been reinvesting all of its profits into the business. This definitely contributes to the high earnings growth number that we discussed above.

Conclusion

In total, we are pretty happy with CytomX Therapeutics' performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. That being so, according to the latest industry analyst forecasts, the company's earnings are expected to shrink in the future. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.title: Does ADX’s Stock or Cash Distribution Choice Reveal a Deeper Shift in Capital Return Strategy?
data.description:
• Adams Diversified Equity Fund, Inc. announced on January 15, 2026, a $0.47 per-share distribution payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 26, 2026, with payment available in additional common shares by default or in cash upon timely election.
  • This optional stock-or-cash structure is particularly important for investors because it lets them tailor the form of their return without changing their holdings outright.
  • We’ll now examine how this flexible stock-or-cash distribution option shapes Adams Diversified Equity Fund’s investment narrative for current and prospective shareholders.

These 10 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Adams Diversified Equity Fund's Investment Narrative?

To own Adams Diversified Equity Fund, you need to be comfortable with a straightforward story: this is an actively managed equity portfolio where returns hinge on how its holdings perform and how efficiently the team runs the closed‑end structure. The big near term catalysts remain the upcoming full‑year 2025 results on February 18, 2026 and any update on how much of recent net income stems from one‑off gains rather than repeatable sources. The fresh $0.47 stock‑or‑cash distribution fits into ADX’s long history of flexible payouts and, by itself, does not appear to change the main drivers of value or risk. If anything, it reinforces ADX’s income profile while keeping attention squarely on discount movements, earnings quality and fee discipline.

However, investors should not overlook how large one‑off gains can complicate the earnings picture. Adams Diversified Equity Fund's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 49%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The single US$46.55 fair value estimate from the Simply Wall St Community points to a very large perceived undervaluation, yet earlier we saw how one‑off gains and an unstable dividend record complicate the story. You are seeing just one angle here, so it is worth comparing this optimism with other views on ADX’s discount behavior, income sustainability and upcoming results before deciding how the fund fits into your portfolio.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To Gilead Sciences (GILD) Trodelvy Keytruda Breast Cancer Data In NEJM
data.description:
• Gilead Sciences announced that The New England Journal of Medicine has published full Phase 3 ASCENT-04/KEYNOTE-D19 results, showing Trodelvy plus Keytruda improved progression-free survival over Keytruda plus chemotherapy in first-line PD-L1+ metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, with no new safety concerns and fewer treatment discontinuations.
  • This positions Trodelvy as an increasingly important oncology asset for Gilead, with four positive Phase 3 trials in HER2- metastatic breast cancer and regulatory submissions now under review in the US and Europe.
  • With the combination therapy’s progression-free survival benefit now in a top-tier journal, we’ll assess how this reshapes Gilead’s investment narrative.

Rare earth metals are an input to most high-tech devices, military and defence systems and electric vehicles. The global race is on to secure supply of these critical minerals. Beat the pack to uncover the 32 best rare earth metal stocks of the very few that mine this essential strategic resource.

Advertisement

What Is Gilead Sciences' Investment Narrative?

To own Gilead today, you really need to believe in a durable HIV and virology cash engine, steadily growing oncology contributions, and disciplined use of the company’s strong free cash flow for bolt‑on deals rather than headline-grabbing megamergers. The newly published ASCENT‑04 Phase 3 data in NEJM strengthens Trodelvy’s case as a core oncology pillar, but the immediate financial impact is more about de‑risking the breast cancer franchise than transforming near-term numbers. In the short term, the key catalysts still look like regulatory decisions on the new TNBC indications, early uptake of Yeztugo in HIV prevention, and any follow-on acquisitions similar to CymaBay. On the risk side, Gilead’s high debt load, a rich CEO pay package relative to peers, and mixed readouts in parts of the Trodelvy program remain important pressure points that this latest news does not fully offset.

However, one risk around Trodelvy’s broader trial program could matter more than it first appears. Gilead Sciences' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To NCC (OM:NCC B) Q4 2025 Guidance And New Public-Sector Contracts
data.description:
• NCC announced that it has been commissioned to expand a police office building in Linköping under a turnkey contract worth about SEK 170 million, while also securing a SEK 175 million school project in Surahammar and issuing earnings guidance for the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • A key insight is that NCC’s new guidance, providing specific expectations for Q4 2025 net sales and operating profit, gives investors clearer visibility into upcoming operational performance alongside its growing portfolio of security-focused and public-sector construction projects.

What Is NCC's Investment Narrative?

To stay invested in NCC, you need to believe in a disciplined, low‑glamour compounder that converts a steady Nordic project pipeline into resilient earnings, rather than rapid growth. The new Q4 2025 guidance, with specific net sales and EBIT figures, tightens near‑term expectations and gives more transparency on profitability at a time when consensus only expected modest earnings growth. The fresh police station and Surahammar school contracts, worth about SEK 345,000,000 combined, add incremental support to the order book but are small relative to quarterly sales, so they are unlikely to shift the big picture on their own. data.title: How Investors May Respond To Urban Edge Properties (UE) Expanding Undrawn US$950 Million Credit Capacity
data.description:
• On January 22, 2026, Urban Edge Properties announced it had entered into US$950 million of unsecured credit facilities, including a US$700 million revolving credit line maturing in June 2030 and US$250 million of delayed-draw term loans with staggered maturities out to January 2033.
  • By expanding borrowing capacity, extending debt maturities, and keeping the facilities undrawn, the company has increased its financial flexibility to fund growth, development and general corporate needs on its own timetable.
  • We’ll now examine how this expanded, flexible credit capacity shapes Urban Edge Properties’ investment narrative and influences perceptions of its future funding plans.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

What Is Urban Edge Properties' Investment Narrative?

To own Urban Edge Properties, you really have to believe in its focused urban retail strategy despite recent earnings pressure, weaker one year returns and a forecast for lower revenue and profit. The new US$950 million unsecured credit facilities fit into that story by giving the REIT more breathing room on debt maturities and undrawn capital to support projects as opportunities arise, without forcing asset sales at awkward times. In the near term, the key catalysts still look tied to leasing progress, rent spreads, and execution on development and redevelopment, but the expanded credit line slightly reduces balance sheet strain where interest coverage has been tight. The flip side is that this flexibility also increases the temptation to add leverage in a business where earnings and interest costs are already under scrutiny.

However, investors should be aware that interest coverage remains a central pressure point.
Urban Edge Properties' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 15%. Find out what it's worth.

Two Simply Wall St Community members see fair value for Urban Edge clustered around US$22.29 to US$22.57 per share, but they are working off forecasts that precede the new US$950 million facility. You are weighing those viewpoints against a business facing earnings pressure, weaker interest cover and more available debt capacity, which could all influence how comfortably Urban Edge can use that credit to support its plans. The diversity of opinions highlights why it is worth comparing multiple perspectives before deciding how this financing really shapes the company’s future performance.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider AutoNation (AN) After A 203% Five Year Rally?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether AutoNation's share price still offers value after a strong run, or if most of the opportunity is already priced in, this article is designed to help you frame that question clearly.
  • AutoNation's stock closed at US$219.08, with returns of 2.2% over the last 7 days, 4.3% over 30 days, 6.1% year to date, 18.3% over 1 year, 86.5% over 3 years and 203.1% over 5 years. This naturally raises questions about what is already reflected in the current valuation.
  • Recent news around AutoNation has focused on its positioning in the US auto retail space and on how investors are thinking about the balance between used and new vehicle exposure, capital allocation and longer term demand trends. This context helps explain why the share price has been in focus for investors who are considering both opportunity and risk.
  • Based on our checks, AutoNation currently has a valuation score of 4 out of 6. Next, we look at how different valuation methods line up with that score, before finishing with a straightforward way to get an even clearer picture of what the stock might be worth.

AutoNation delivered 18.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Specialty Retail industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: AutoNation Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to a present value. For AutoNation, this is done using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model that focuses on cash available to shareholders.

On a last twelve month basis, AutoNation reported free cash flow of $251.78 million outflow, so the model relies heavily on forward projections. Analysts and extrapolations point to free cash flow reaching $886 million in 2030, with a series of annual estimates between 2026 and 2035 that gradually adjust over time based on analyst inputs and Simply Wall St extrapolation beyond the usual 5 year window.

Bringing all of those projected cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of about $230.86 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $219.08, the DCF implies AutoNation trades at roughly a 5.1% discount, which is a modest gap rather than a dramatic one.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

AutoNation is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for AutoNation.

Approach 2: AutoNation Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like AutoNation, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see how much investors are currently paying for each dollar of earnings. It links the share price directly to earnings, which is usually the main driver of long term returns for shareholders.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often line up with a lower P/E.

AutoNation currently trades on a P/E of about 12.05x. That sits below the broader Specialty Retail industry average of about 20.51x and below the peer average of roughly 13.08x, which may catch your eye if you are comparing it directly with other retailers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework goes a step further. It estimates what P/E might be reasonable for AutoNation given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, its industry and market cap, and company specific risks. Because it ties the multiple to the company’s own profile rather than just broad group averages, it can be a more tailored reference point.

On this basis, AutoNation’s Fair Ratio is 15.67x, above the current 12.05x, which suggests the shares may be undervalued on earnings.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1427 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your AutoNation Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, which help you connect your view of AutoNation’s business to a clear financial story.

A Narrative is simply your story behind the numbers, where you set out what you think could happen to revenue, earnings and margins, then link that to an assumed fair value for the stock.

In practice, a Narrative ties three things together: the company’s story, a forward looking forecast and a resulting estimate of fair value that you can set against today’s share price.

You can build and compare Narratives directly on Simply Wall St, inside the Community page used by millions of investors, and use them as a quick reference for whether fair value sits above or below the current price when you are thinking about buying or selling.

Because Narratives refresh when new information such as earnings reports or news is added to the platform, your view of AutoNation can evolve without you rebuilding your work from scratch.

For example, one AutoNation Narrative might assume a conservative outlook and arrive at a lower fair value, while another, more optimistic Narrative could use higher revenue and margin assumptions and land on a meaningfully higher figure.

Do you think there's more to the story for AutoNation? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Cactus Weighs Baker Hughes Deal Benefits Against Steel Tariff Pressures
data.description:
• Cactus, Inc. (NYSE:WHD) announces an acquisition of a controlling interest in Baker Hughes’ Surface Pressure Control business.
  • The transaction is framed by the company as a transformative step that significantly expands its scale and capabilities.
  • Cactus also addresses the impact of a sudden increase in steel import tariffs on margins and supply chain planning.

Cactus, Inc. (NYSE:WHD) focuses on wellhead and pressure control equipment and related services for energy producers, so taking control of Baker Hughes’ Surface Pressure Control business directly ties into its core offerings. For readers tracking oilfield equipment suppliers, this move reshapes how Cactus is positioned across surface wellhead, valves, and related after market support. At the same time, higher steel import tariffs touch a key input cost, keeping attention on how the company handles procurement and pricing decisions.

For investors, the combination of a large acquisition and tariff pressures raises questions about integration, capital allocation, and cost discipline over the coming years. How Cactus manages these parallel shifts could influence its competitive footing, contract terms with customers, and the balance between growth spending and financial flexibility.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Cactus by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Cactus.

How Cactus stacks up against its biggest competitors

The acquisition of a controlling interest in Baker Hughes’ Surface Pressure Control business gives Cactus a much larger installed base across wellhead and pressure control equipment, which could deepen its presence with major operators and strengthen its aftermarket revenue stream. At the same time, the doubling of Section 232 steel tariffs directly affects a core input for this expanded footprint. The success of the deal will likely hinge on how well Cactus aligns procurement, pricing, and integration plans across both its existing Pressure Control segment and the acquired assets.

Advertisement

Cactus narrative, growth ambitions meet real world costs

Recent options activity and mixed analyst sentiment suggest the market is already braced for bigger swings in Cactus’ share price. This acquisition plus the tariff change gives a clear fundamental reason why views might be polarized. For those who see Cactus as a consolidator in wellhead and pressure control, the deal fits a growth-focused narrative. At the same time, the higher steel costs and earnings estimate revisions support a more cautious narrative that focuses on execution risk and cost pressure.

Risks and rewards to keep on your radar

  • Larger scale in surface pressure control equipment and services that could improve Cactus’ relevance with key customers.
  • Management confidence signaled through an 8% increase in the quarterly dividend, pointing to comfort with free cash flow after the deal.
  • Higher steel tariffs that may pressure margins in the Pressure Control segment if cost recovery through pricing or efficiencies is limited.
  • Integration and capital allocation questions around absorbing Baker Hughes’ business while analysts are trimming earnings expectations.

What to watch next

From here, it will be important to watch how Cactus talks about integration milestones, tariff cost pass through, and dividend sustainability over the next few quarters, and how that lines up with market expectations already visible in options pricing. If you want to see how other investors are thinking about this story, you can read community views in this narrative hub.

data.title: Navitas Refocuses On AI Data Center Power As Stock Momentum Builds
data.description:
• Navitas Semiconductor (NasdaqGM:NVTS) is exiting its low margin consumer chip business to focus on AI data center power solutions.
  • The company is prioritizing next generation architectures, including 800V platforms aimed at large scale computing workloads.
  • This shift represents a material change in how Navitas allocates resources across its product portfolio.

For investors watching the AI supply chain, this move positions Navitas within the broader discussion around power technology for high performance computing. The shares recently closed at $11.29, with the stock up 12.9% over the past week, 47.4% over the past month, and 237.0% over the past year. These returns indicate that expectations around the company have changed meaningfully in a relatively short period of time.

By stepping away from lower margin consumer chips and focusing on AI data centers, Navitas is tying its strategy more closely to large, power-hungry compute platforms such as 800V architectures. For investors, key questions include how effectively the company can execute in this more focused segment and how its technology is adopted as major chipmakers adjust their power requirements.

data.title: Lucid Group (LCID) Deepens Rockwell Ties: Can Saudi Manufacturing Scale Sharpen Its EV Cost Advantage?
data.description:
• Rockwell Automation recently announced it will deploy its FactoryTalk manufacturing execution software and provide local support at Lucid Group’s new Saudi Arabia plant, aiming to improve production visibility, efficiency, and workforce training in line with Saudi Vision 2030.
  • This deepened collaboration highlights how software-driven manufacturing and local talent development are becoming central to Lucid’s international expansion and long-term scaling ambitions.
  • We’ll now examine how this enhanced Rockwell partnership in Saudi Arabia shapes Lucid’s investment narrative, particularly around manufacturing scale and operational efficiency.

We've found 12 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Lucid Group's Investment Narrative?

To own Lucid today, you really have to believe it can turn rapid revenue growth and an expanding product lineup into a sustainable, scaled manufacturing business before its cash runway runs short. The deepened Rockwell Automation partnership in Saudi Arabia speaks directly to that belief: it reinforces Lucid’s pitch that software-led, highly automated plants can support Gravity, the midsize platform and even the robotaxi program with Nuro and Uber. In the near term, the Rockwell news mostly tweaks the story around key catalysts rather than rewriting it. Q4 2025 results on February 24, production ramp metrics, and any funding or Saudi-related capacity updates still look like the main share price drivers. The core risk remains that Lucid’s negative gross margins and limited cash runway outpace any manufacturing and demand progress, even with better factory software.

However, one issue could quickly outweigh the excitement around new software and Saudi expansion. Our valuation report here indicates Lucid Group may be overvalued.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s 16 fair value estimates for Lucid span roughly US$1 to over US$28 per share, reflecting sharply different views on its potential. Set that beside the company’s very large annual net loss and short cash runway, and it becomes clear why opinions diverge so much around how quickly operational gains from Saudi and Gravity can translate into a more resilient business.

data.title: Heliostar Metals (TSXV:HSTR) Valuation Check After 2026 Production Guidance And Investor Forum Plans
data.description:
Heliostar Metals (TSXV:HSTR) has moved into focus after releasing 2026 production guidance of 50,000 to 55,000 ounces of gold, along with plans to present at the Metals Investor Forum in Vancouver on January 23.

See our latest analysis for Heliostar Metals.

The 9.63% 30 day share price return and 62.64% 90 day share price return suggest momentum has been building into the 2026 production guidance and upcoming Metals Investor Forum appearance, while the very large 1 year total shareholder return contrasts with a still developing longer term 5 year total shareholder return of 94.74%.

If this kind of move in a single miner has your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Heliostar now pointing to 2026 production of 50,000 to 55,000 ounces and trading at a steep discount to a CA$5.45 target, you have to ask: is this a genuine entry point, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Preferred P/E of 18.8x: Is it justified?

Heliostar Metals is trading on a P/E of 18.8x, which sits below the wider Canadian Metals and Mining industry average of 26x but screens as expensive versus its immediate peer group.

The P/E multiple compares the current share price to earnings per share and is a simple way to see how much investors are paying for each unit of profit. For a miner that has only recently moved into profitability, this kind of multiple often reflects how the market weighs early earnings against expectations for future production and margin trends.

On one hand, Heliostar is described as good value when set against the broader Canadian Metals and Mining industry on a P/E basis, suggesting the market price is not stretching that wider sector benchmark. On the other hand, compared with its peer average P/E of about 25x in the negative range, the stock is described as expensive, which hints that investors are assigning a richer multiple than some directly comparable names even though fair ratio data is not available to show where this might settle over time.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-Earnings of 18.8x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, you also need to weigh execution risk at Ana Paula, as well as the possibility that the current P/E and strong recent returns already reflect much of the 2026 story.

data.title: Assessing Cencora (COR) Valuation After Strong Returns And Premium P/E Multiple
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Cencora is on investors’ radar today

Cencora (COR) has drawn attention after a solid year of positive total returns, along with reported annual revenue of US$321.3b and net income of US$1.6b, prompting investors to reassess how the stock is currently priced.

See our latest analysis for Cencora.

The current share price of US$352.47 sits against a 30 day share price return of 3.5% and a 90 day share price return of 5.9%, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 43.3% and 5 year total shareholder return of 248.5% point to strong longer term momentum that investors are weighing against today’s valuation.

If Cencora’s run has you thinking about where else growth and resilience might show up next, it could be a good moment to scan quality US healthcare names through healthcare stocks.

With Cencora trading at US$352.47, a reported intrinsic discount of about 49% and a price target of US$396.80, the key question is whether there is still an opportunity here or if markets are already pricing in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 9.1% Undervalued

The most followed narrative puts Cencora’s fair value at about $387.93 per share versus the last close of $352.47, framing the recent share price strength against a slightly higher long term view backed by detailed model assumptions.

Cencora's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure and advanced analytics positions the company to capitalize on the accelerating digitization of healthcare and regulatory requirements like the Drug Supply Chain Security Act, improving supply chain efficiency and transparency, which should drive higher net margins and operating income over time.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that projected uplift in margins and earnings? The narrative leans on steady top line expansion, richer profit mix, and a future valuation multiple that assumes Cencora keeps earning its place in premium territory. The details of how those pieces fit together are where the story really gets interesting.

Result: Fair Value of $387.93 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that story can change quickly if biosimilars and generics keep pressuring fees, or if international weakness and regulatory scrutiny place greater pressure on profitability.

Another Angle on Cencora’s Valuation

There is a twist when you look at Cencora through its P/E. The shares trade at about 44x earnings, compared with roughly 23.6x for the wider US Healthcare industry and 22.9x for peers, while the fair ratio is estimated at 36.9x.

That gap suggests investors are already paying a clear premium, even though some models still flag Cencora as trading about 49.4% below estimated fair value. The key issue is whether you view that premium as justified by potential future earnings strength, or as a valuation risk if expectations cool.
